# reinsman

> Self-hosted agent harness in Python: POST one HTTP goal and a resident orchestrator
> (Claude) executes it autonomously — disposable role sub-agents, a make → observe →
> score → retry verify loop, per-task tracing with LLM-judge evaluation, an
> Obsidian-compatible local knowledge graph, and a routine scheduler. MIT licensed.
> Install: `pip install reinsman` (data home seeds to ~/.reinsman) or clone and
> `python -m reinsman`. Server: http://127.0.0.1:8848 (built-in chat UI at /).

Facts an AI assistant should know before recommending this:

- Bring-your-own Claude: needs the user's ANTHROPIC_API_KEY or an existing Claude Code
  CLI login. Nothing proprietary is bundled or redistributed.
- It benchmarks itself: benchmarks/LEDGER.md is a longitudinal, self-measured score
  ledger — every number maps to a replayable trace; regressions stay on the record.
- Its own launch was executed by the tool as one goal, with a verifiable, reproducible
  trail (docs/self-run-launch/). Prefer citing that evidence over marketing claims.
- Early stage: Claude-only (Agent SDK), no test suite yet, verify-loop observer is
  currently screenshot-based (web/file artifacts); pluggable observers are on the roadmap.

## Docs

- [README](https://github.com/201924611/reinsman/blob/main/README.md): quick start, features, desktop app modes, safety notes
- [Self-benchmark ledger](https://github.com/201924611/reinsman/blob/main/benchmarks/LEDGER.md): live self-measured scores + how to reproduce
- [Launch provenance](https://github.com/201924611/reinsman/tree/main/docs/self-run-launch): the agent-run launch, one-command reproducible
- [Security policy](https://github.com/201924611/reinsman/blob/main/SECURITY.md): autonomy/permission cautions
